name: Teable
tagline: A spreadsheet-style database app builder backed by Postgres
metaDescription: Teable is an open-source, no-code database app builder with a spreadsheet UI, backed by Postgres. Self-host the Airtable alternative with Docker.
overview: Teable is a no-code database app builder that uses a spreadsheet-like interface to create database applications. It is backed by Postgres, so your data lives in a real relational database that you can also query with SQL while non-technical users work in a familiar grid.

It is built for teams that want the ease of a spreadsheet without outgrowing it. The project advertises real-time collaboration and scaling to millions of rows, and it can be self-hosted so your data stays under your control instead of sitting only with a cloud provider.

In the low-code / no-code space, Teable positions itself as a self-hostable Airtable alternative: spreadsheet-style editing on top of Postgres, multiple data views, and a plugin system for extending the app.
features: Spreadsheet-style grid editing on top of a Postgres database, with direct SQL query access
Multiple views of the same data: Grid, Form, Kanban, Gallery, and Calendar
Built-in fields and data tools: formulas, filtering, sorting, grouping, aggregation, and validation
Real-time collaboration, comments, record history, and undo/redo
Import/export, batch editing, attachments preview, and charts
Plugin system plus an SDK for building extensions

gettingStarted.intro: The quickest way to run Teable yourself is the Docker Compose example in the repository; the development setup below is for working on Teable itself.

steps.heading: Deploy with Docker
steps.body: From a clone of the repo, change into the standalone example directory and bring the stack up in the background. See the official docs for configuration details.

steps.heading: Install dependencies for development
steps.body: Enable Corepack so the correct package manager is used, then install the project dependencies with pnpm.

steps.heading: Initialize Postgres
steps.body: Set up the database in development mode using the provided make target.

steps.heading: Run the dev server
steps.body: Start the backend; it launches the Next.js frontend automatically, and file changes auto-reload.

useCases: Give non-technical teammates a spreadsheet UI while keeping data in Postgres for SQL access and reporting
Self-host an Airtable-style app so business data stays on your own infrastructure
Manage large operational datasets (toward millions of rows) that outgrow a typical spreadsheet
Track projects or content across Kanban, Calendar, Gallery, and Form views over a single shared table
